hero.title: SUPERGLAZKA
hero.subtitle: Interactive comic about eye health. Save planet Videal together with the superheroine!

about.title: About the comic
about.subtitle: In a magical world, eye health is the main superpower
about.p1: Far, far away in the Universe there is a planet Videal — a world where every resident devotes themselves to protecting the eyesight of children on Earth. But one day the Great Darkness threatens to consume the planet...
about.p2: From an ancient ritual appears a girl with huge blue eyes — Superglazka. In a red suit with a white cape, she goes on a journey to save Videal and teach children to take care of their eyes.
about.p3: Together with loyal friends — Vanya, Wise Crystal and Advisor — she goes through exciting adventures, mini-games and eye trainings. Every frame is voiced, every choice matters!

features.title: Features
features.subtitle: What makes Superglazka a unique project for children

comic.title: Interactive comic
comic.desc: Every frame is a full-screen panel with dialogues, voice acting and video inserts. Scroll like stories!

games.title: Mini-games
games.desc: Train your eyes with the heroes: runner, gymnastics, blinking and battle with the villain Lenivus!

voice.title: Voice acting
voice.desc: Professional audio narration of every frame. Listen to the story like an audio play!

video.title: Video
video.desc: AI-generated video inserts bring comic frames to life and immerse you in the atmosphere.

gamesSection.title: Mini-games
gamesSection.subtitle: Train your eyes while playing — each game develops useful skills

runner.title: Pixel Chase
runner.desc: Run, jump and collect bonuses. Trains quick reaction and gaze concentration.

gym.title: Battle with Lenivus
gym.desc: Use three super eye attacks: Laser, Aim and Tears. Defeat the villain!

blink.title: Eye Gymnastics
blink.desc: Blink, squint and open your eyes wide. Strengthens eye muscles.

tracker.title: Follow the Ball
tracker.desc: Follow the moving ball with your eyes. Develops smooth eye movements.

faq.title: FAQ
faq.subtitle: Answers to the most popular questions about Superglazka, eye health and games
faq.a1: Superglazka is a free interactive educational comic about eye health for children aged 5–12. The child immerses in the story of planet Videal, plays mini-games and learns how to take care of vision.
faq.q2: What age is Superglazka suitable for?
faq.a2: The comic is designed for children from 5 to 12 years old. Younger readers can listen to narration, while older ones can read dialogues and complete tasks independently.
faq.a3: Yes, the basic version of the comic and all mini-games are completely free. Additional episodes and cosmetic items are available by subscription.
faq.a4: Open vidial-media.ru in a browser on your computer, tablet or phone. Press 'Start' and dive into the story. To save progress, simply register or log in as a guest.
faq.a5: It is a simple rule for preventing eye fatigue: every 20 minutes of near work, look at an object 20 feet (≈6 meters) away for 20 seconds. It helps relax the eye muscles.
faq.a6: The project features several mini-games: blink training, eye gymnastics, obstacle running and quest search. Each game teaches useful habits through fun interaction.
faq.q7: How to prevent myopia in a child?
faq.a7: Key measures: follow the 20-20-20 rule, walk outdoors for at least 1.5 hours a day, play sports (badminton, swimming), ensure proper lighting when reading and have regular eye check-ups.
faq.q8: Does Superglazka work on a phone?
faq.a8: Yes, the site is fully adapted for smartphones and tablets. You can install it as a PWA app via the browser menu 'Add to Home Screen'.
faq.q9: Where to find eye health tips?
faq.a9: All useful materials are collected in the Blog section on the website, as well as in the parents' checklist. There you will find the 20-20-20 rule, eye gymnastics, nutrition and daily routine tips.
faq.q10: How to contact the authors of Superglazka?
faq.a10: Write to us through the feedback form on the website or in the official VK and Telegram communities. We are always happy to receive feedback, suggestions and ideas!

checklistPage.title: 10 Eye Health Rules
checklistPage.subtitle: Checklist for parents · Print and hang on the fridge
checklistPage.rule202020: 20-20-20 Rule. Every 20 minutes of screen time — 20 seconds rest, look at 6 meters.
checklistPage.distance: Screen distance. Minimum 50–60 cm to monitor, 30 cm to tablet.
checklistPage.lighting: Good lighting. No glare on the screen, enough light in the room.
checklistPage.blink: Blink more! Remind your child to blink during screen work.
checklistPage.noScreenBeforeBed: No screens 1 hour before bed. Blue light disrupts melatonin production.
checklistPage.vitaminA: Vitamin A. Carrots, pumpkin, apricots, liver — daily in the diet.
checklistPage.omega3: Omega-3. Fish, flax seeds, walnuts — protect the retina.
checklistPage.walks: Walks in fresh air. At least 1 hour a day — natural eye training.
checklistPage.doctor: Ophthalmologist checkup. Once a year even without complaints.
checklistPage.noRub: Don't rub eyes with dirty hands. Infections are a common cause of vision problems.

controls.question: How to control?
controls.answer: On computer: use up/down arrows or Space key to go to the next frame. Esc key hides/shows the interface.
controls.answer2: On phone: swipe up/down to switch frames. Tap the screen to pause the video.

sound.question: What to do if sound doesn't work?
sound.answer: 1. Check that the required audio tracks are enabled in settings. 2. On iOS, sound only turns on after the user's first touch — this is a Safari limitation. 3. Check the device volume and whether silent mode is on. 4. Reload the page — sometimes the browser blocks autoplay.

coins.question: How to earn coins?
coins.answer: Coins are awarded for completing frames and mini-games. The better the game result (more stars), the more coins. You can also get bonus coins for replaying a chapter.

locked.question: Why is the chapter locked?
locked.answer: Chapters open sequentially. To unlock the next chapter, you need to finish watching the previous one. Progress is saved automatically.

subtitles.question: How to turn on subtitles?
subtitles.answer: Open settings → 'Subtitles' section → toggle the switch. You can also choose font size: small, medium or large.

theme.question: How to change theme?
theme.answer: In settings → 'Interface' section → 'Dark theme' toggle. The choice is saved and works on both pages.

checklist.title: 10 Eye Health Rules
checklist.subtitle: Parent checklist · Print and hang on the fridge
checklist.item0: 20-20-20 Rule. Every 20 minutes of screen time — 20 seconds rest, look at 6 meters.
checklist.item1: Screen distance. Minimum 50–60 cm to monitor, 30 cm to tablet.
checklist.item2: Good lighting. No glare on screen, enough light in the room.
checklist.item3: Blink more! Remind your child to blink while using screens.
checklist.item4: No screens 1 hour before sleep. Blue light disrupts melatonin production.
checklist.item5: Vitamin A. Carrots, pumpkin, apricots, liver — daily in diet.
checklist.item6: Omega-3. Fish, flax seeds, walnuts — protect the retina.
checklist.item7: Walks in fresh air. Minimum 1 hour a day — natural eye training.
checklist.item8: Eye doctor checkup. Once a year even without complaints.
checklist.item9: Do not rub eyes with dirty hands. Infections are a common cause of vision problems.
